mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining federal funds rates indicates strong consumer demand supported by accommodative financing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression, or combined signal accuracy drops below 50% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in expansion regimes, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and higher household income, which drives consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.5 million units, construction activity and related supply chains contract sharply, signaling a demand downturn that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered periods, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with Kruskal-Wallis p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stagnant or rising federal funds rates forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during stable macroeconomic environments.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the signal accuracy drops below 45% during periods of high macroeconomic volatility.
